name: Joan Mateo Duarte Politi
positioning: I build products where AI, systems, and interface design meet.
story: I'm from Argentina, and I've been solving problems since I can remember. As a kid I was the one going to math olympics, not because someone pushed me into it, but because I genuinely loved the challenge. The solution is never the hard part. The approach is everything.

At 21 I dropped out of university to build a crypto mining company. It wasn't a startup in the traditional sense, but it was a new sector, it was futuristic, and we were making money. We scaled fast. That itch to build a real startup never left, so we created DL3ARN, then I taught myself to code because I refused to keep delegating the most important part of a startup.

I don't specialize in one layer of the stack. I specialize in the full picture: understanding what needs to be built, why it matters, and making it real. Flare's architecture has 16 database tables with row-level security, 13 Edge Functions, 6 AI agents, and 12 database triggers. I built all of it.
The common thread across everything I build is conviction. I pick problems I believe in, go deep, and ship.

chess.text: With only 16 pieces and 64 squares, there are more possible positions than grains of sand on Earth. Pattern recognition, calculated risk, knowing when to trade material for position. It maps directly to building products.
